item.title: Waller, Welcoming Remarks on the International Role of the U.S. Dollar

rating.summary: Governor Waller delivered brief welcoming remarks at the Federal Reserve's Fifth Conference on the International Roles of the U.S. Dollar, focusing on how digital assets and stablecoins are reshaping the dollar's global role. The remarks outlined the conference research agenda covering stablecoin payment systems, spillovers into financial markets, and links to U.S. Treasury demand. No monetary policy views or rate guidance were expressed.
rating.stance_rationale: This is a ceremonial/conference introduction speech with no discussion of interest rates, inflation, employment, or monetary policy stance. It is entirely focused on the international role of the dollar and digital asset innovation.
key_quotes: Technological innovation is increasingly altering how households and businesses interact with dollars, whether it is through holding new types of assets or through changes to the payment rails by which dollar-denominated assets are transferred, intermediated, and settled.
As an economist, I believe that is a good thing—more competition generally leads to better outcomes for both consumers and society as a whole.
